pairs.question: Who was Alexander I and what was his reign period as King of Greece?
pairs.answer: Alexander I was King of Greece from 1917 until his death in 1920. He ascended to the throne after his father, King Constantine XII, was forced to abdicate.

pairs.question: Why did Alexander I become king instead of his older brother, Crown Prince George?
pairs.answer: Alexander became king because his older brother, Crown Prince George, was deemed not yet ready to rule at the time of their father's abdication.

pairs.question: What significant territorial changes occurred during Alexander I's reign?
pairs.answer: During Alexander I's reign, Greece's territorial extent increased significantly following the Entente's victory in World War I and the early stages of the Greco-Turkish War of 1919–1922.

pairs.question: How did Alexander I's reign end?
pairs.answer: Alexander I died on 25 October 1920 at the age of 27 after being bitten by a Barbary macaque at his palace in Athens. The injury became infected, leading to sepsis.

pairs.question: What happened in the election held shortly after Alexander I's death?
pairs.answer: An election was held to decide whether to restore Alexander's father, Ex-king Constantine XII, to the throne. Nearly 99% of the votes favored his return, and Constantine was restored to the throne just two months after Alexander's death.

pairs.question: What was the Greek government's view of Alexander I during his reign?
pairs.answer: The Greek government considered Alexander I a puppet king and even attempted to imprison him at his own palace, though their efforts failed.

pairs.question: What conflicts did Greece participate in during Alexander I's reign?
pairs.answer: During Alexander I's reign, Greece supported troops in wars against the Ottoman Empire and Bulgaria, as part of the broader context of World War I and the Greco-Turkish War of 1919–1922.
